text:
e refers to the elder sage as 'Bhagavan Garga' as Vrddha-
garga and as Gar gem maharfind. Incidentally the Gargasarfihitd is also
known as Vrddhagargiya. The Mahdbhdrata too refers to the Elder sage.
With regard to the identity ofKaSyapa and Kdiyapa, we may conclude
that the two names may, after all, point to one and the same
person. For, Utpala refers to the author's difference of opinion with
Kaiyapa and quotes in support KdSyapa. The difference may be
due to the carelessness of scribes.
Regarding Devala and others too some interesting details can
be called from Utpala's Vivrti:
(i) It is quite evident from P.S.I. 22 that Varaha has given
the effects of planetary years, etc. on the authority of ancient sages
(Munipranitam dlokya). Hence it is intriguing why Utpala has not
quoted the authority of sages like PardSara, but quoted only a single
verse of Devala. On the other hand, he quotes eight verses from
each of Yavaneivara and the author's Samdsa-sarhhitd. This leads us
